factors.name: US CPI surprise (HOT)
factors.summary: April CPI came in at 3.8% YoY vs 3.7% consensus (BBC, MarketWatch), driven by Iran war-induced energy and food inflation. This is the highest level since May 2023 and surprised to the upside, immediately pushing gold lower and bolstering the USD.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS implicit)
factors.summary: 10-year nominal Treasury yield jumped to ~4.45% (Investinglive) on the hot CPI print. With inflation now 3.8%, real yields have likely steepened, directly weighing on gold. Higher real rates are the single most correlated bearish driver for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY proxy)
factors.summary: USD/JPY rallied to 157.65, USD/CAD jumped to 1.3715, and EUR/USD broke below both 100-hour and 200-hour MAs, now at ~1.1743 (FXStreet). Strong greenback on inflation surprise directly competes with gold demand.

factors.name: Fed hawkish pivot risk
factors.summary: Fed's Goolsbee (Chicago) stated bluntly 'We have an inflation problem in this country' (FXStreet), signaling hawkish concern. Morgan Stanley Chief US Economist Gapen expects inflation to peak May/June but noted this will keep the Fed 'on the sidelines' — however, the surprise upside print raises odds of higher-for-longer rate expectations.

factors.name: Geopolitical premium (Iran war)
factors.summary: Trump stated Iran ceasefire is 'on life support' (MarketWatch), oil topped $100/bbl again, and Middle East tensions remain acute. This typically supports gold as a safe-haven hedge, but the inflation-via-energy channel is dominating sentiment today.

factors.name: Risk-off equity selloff
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 1.45%, S&P 500 down 0.80%, Russell 2000 down 0.83% (Investinglive, MarketWatch). Broad equities weakness typically supports gold safe-haven, but the dominant driver is the inflation shock and higher real yields, which is overriding traditional flight-to-safety flows.

factors.name: Momentum/positioning
factors.summary: Gold has 'extended its slide on Tuesday' after the hot CPI, retracing prior day's gains, per FXStreet. Retail AI traders are rotating out of risk; profit-taking in speculative longs amid momentum reversal in equities (chip stocks down 13%+) is adding liquidation pressure on gold.

factors.name: Fed rate path expectations
factors.summary: Hot inflation surprises historically extend the timeline for rate cuts. Markets are repricing for 'higher-for-longer' rates, which lifts real yields and terminal rate expectations — bearish for gold.

factors.name: Silver weakness (correlated asset)
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) declined 2.18% to ~$84.10 (FXStreet), breaking a 4-day winning streak. Precious metals broadly are under pressure from nominal yield rises and USD strength.

factors.name: Copper resilience despite fears
factors.summary: Copper rallied to $14,000/ton, near all-time highs (Bloomberg), defying Iran war concerns. Suggests some demand-side resilience and risk appetite underneath, partially offsetting safe-haven flows into gold.

key_levels.support: 4,650 (recent swing low before today's drop), 4,600 (medium-term support)

screening.narrative: Gold has broken lower on a hotter-than-expected April CPI print (3.8% vs 3.7% consensus), which has caused US Treasury yields to spike and the USD to rally across major pairs. The real-yield shock — the single biggest driver of gold — is now negative for the yellow metal, as inflation came in hot but NOT so hot as to force an immediate Fed capitulation. Instead, markets are repricing for higher-for-longer rates, eroding gold's appeal. While Iran war tensions and a modest equity selloff provide some safe-haven tailwind, the nominal yield backdrop and strong dollar are overwhelming these flows. Positioning momentum has shifted to the downside.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation print (if scheduled for this week — would confirm or refute the CPI upside surprise)
Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Waller, Powell commentary on inflation outlook and rate path)
Trump-Xi meeting in Beijing (tariff/trade negotiations could shift risk sentiment and inflation expectations)
Weekly jobless claims / employment data (secondary confirmation of labor market vs inflation trade-off)
Middle East ceasefire developments (real-time news on Iran tensions — any major escalation could reverse safe-haven flows)
screening.risk_warning: Main downside risk to this SELL bias: (1) Rapid pivot by Fed/Powell to dovish tone suggesting rate cuts despite inflation surprise (would tank real yields and reverse the move). (2) Significant escalation in Iran-US conflict causing oil spike >$110 and/or broader geopolitical contagion, triggering flight-to-safety that overrides real-yield headwinds. (3) Unexpected weakness in US economic data (ISM, payrolls) that forces rate-cut expectations higher despite inflation, inverting the yield curve further and supporting gold. (4) Large CB gold buying announcement (PBoC, RBI, etc.) that revives structural demand narrative. Monitor real yields and DXY above all; if 10y TIPS yield falls below +2.0% or DXY breaks below 106.00, bias flips NEUTRAL or BULLISH.

factors.name: US CPI Inflation
factors.summary: April CPI rose 3.8% YoY vs 3.7% expected (BBC/multiple sources), hotter than forecast. Morgan Stanley Chief Economist Gapen expects inflation to peak May/June, keeping Fed sidelined rest of 2026. Hot inflation erodes Fed dovish bets.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: FXStreet reports strong US CPI lifts USD and Treasury yields, with gold dropping below $4,700 as Dollar strengthens. EUR/USD fell ~0.35% to 1.1743, breaking below 200-hour MA. Stronger DXY is primary headwind for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Treasury yields pushing toward 5% on high inflation, with 10-year near 4.45% per Investinglive. Higher nominal yields and likely higher real yields are strongly negative for non-yielding gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Outlook
factors.summary: Fed's Goolsbee stated 'we have an inflation problem in this country' (FXStreet). Morgan Stanley expects Fed on sidelines rest of 2026. Hot CPI reinforces higher-for-longer stance, removing dovish pivot hopes that support gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Iran ceasefire 'on life support' per Trump (multiple sources), US crude tops $100 again, oil rallying on prolonged Middle East conflict fears. Typically safe-haven supportive for gold, but currently being outweighed by USD/yields.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI above $100 due to Iran tensions and potential Hormuz disruption (Vietnam urging US to let tankers through blockade per Bloomberg). Inflationary for headline CPI (bullish gold long-term), but strengthens USD and hawkish Fed expectations (bearish gold near-term).

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Nasdaq down -1.45%, S&P -0.80%, chip stocks hit hard (Qualcomm -13%). Flight to USD over gold; momentum stocks selling suggests profit-taking not panic flight-to-quality that would favor gold over Dollar.

factors.name: FX Volatility Comments
factors.summary: US Treasury Secretary Bessent said excess FX volatility undesirable (FXStreet), supporting JPY slightly. No direct gold impact but indicates official concern over disorderly FX moves.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh news in 24h window on PBoC, RBI, or other major CB purchases. Structural bid remains but not a catalyst today.

factors.name: Copper/Industrial Metals
factors.summary: Copper rallied above $14,000/ton nearing record (Bloomberg), driven by China demand and supply risks. Indicates risk-on in commodities but gold diverging due to USD/yield dynamics.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is extending losses below $4,700 as hotter-than-expected US CPI (3.8% vs 3.7%) drives a strong USD rally and pushes Treasury yields toward 5%. The inflation surprise reinforces Fed higher-for-longer expectations, with officials acknowledging an 'inflation problem' and Morgan Stanley seeing no cuts through 2026. While Iran war tensions and $100+ oil provide geopolitical safe-haven support, the bearish yield/Dollar dynamic is dominating. EUR/USD breaking key moving averages and broad risk-off in equities (Nasdaq -1.45%) confirm Dollar bid outweighing safe-haven flows into gold.
catalysts_ahead: Trump-Xi Beijing summit this week (geopolitical/trade implications)
Further FedSpeak clarifying policy stance post-CPI
Iran ceasefire negotiations outcome (oil price impact)
Next key US data: retail sales, jobless claims later this week
Watch for any shifts in 10-year real yields (TIPS) as dominant gold driver
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran ceasefire breakthrough causes oil crash and USD pullback; (2) unexpected dovish Fed pivot or softer upcoming data (retail sales, claims); (3) flight-to-quality accelerates on equity crash, favoring gold over USD; (4) 10-year real yields reverse lower. Geopolitical escalation beyond current pricing could also trigger safe-haven bid overpowering yield headwinds.

screening.current_price: ~2350-2370 (estimated based on typical April-May levels post-CPI surprise)

factors.name: US Inflation (CPI) Data Release
factors.summary: April CPI came in at 3.8% vs expectations, with energy costs surging from Iran war (BBC 18:36, 18:02). This is HOT inflation, which typically pressures gold initially by raising real yields and supporting Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield rose sharply post-CPI; 10-year notes auctioned at 4.468% (highest since July 2025). Higher nominal yields + hot inflation = higher real yields, which is STRONGLY bearish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strengthened notably post-CPI surprise; GBP/USD fell 0.65% as sellers took control of major FX pairs. Stronger USD is inverse to gold, thus bearish.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Chair Transition
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed to Fed Board (51-45) with Chair vote scheduled for Wed/Thu before Powell's term ends May 15. Policy trajectory still uncertain; Goolsbee warned inflation going 'wrong way' and services inflation a worry, tempering rate-cut enthusiasm.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran War)
factors.summary: Iran-US tensions persist with oil at $100+/bbl, Strait of Hormuz blockade, and ceasefire on 'life support' per Trump (17:55). Persistent conflict backdrop supports safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Crude rallied 3%+ on Tuesday to $100+ amid Hormuz supply concerns and Iran shipment halts (17:39, 17:32). Higher energy inflation supports gold's inflation-hedge narrative but may paradoxically push Fed hawkishness (mixed).

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Traders see 2-in-3 odds inflation hits 4.5% and ~40% odds of 5%+ by year-end (18:21). Market inflation expectations rising = higher nominal yields & real yields when combined with Fed tightening bias.

factors.name: Equity Market Sentiment & Risk-Off
factors.summary: Nasdaq fell 1.45%, S&P 500 down 0.80%, Dow down 0.42% on profit-taking and inflation fears. Flight-to-safety typically aids gold, but the REAL driver is whether USD strengthens (it did post-CPI)—DXY wins vs gold in this regime.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh CB gold buying news in this 24h window; structural CB bid remains neutral baseline, not a fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Services Inflation & Wage Pressures
factors.summary: Fed's Goolsbee noted services inflation 'going the wrong way' and was surprised by upside (17:26). United Airlines flight attendants ratified 31% raises (18:45). Stronger services/wage data = Fed hawkishness = higher real yields = gold headwind.

factors.name: Tariff Refund & Fiscal Dynamics
factors.summary: Tariff refunds beginning post-Supreme Court ruling (18:43); April budget surplus $215B vs $220B expected (18:10). Mixed fiscal signals, minor impact on gold vs rate environment.

key_levels.support: ~2300-2320 (if DXY continues higher and real yields steepen)
key_levels.resistance: ~2380-2400 (if Iran escalates and safe-haven demand overwhelms Fed hawkishness)

screening.narrative: Gold faces conflicting pressures post-April CPI surprise (3.8% vs expected, energy-driven). The hot inflation print initially pushed 10Y Treasury yields higher and strengthened USD—both bearish for gold—despite simultaneous safe-haven bid from Iran war tensions and oil surge. The real yield axis dominates: stronger nominal yields combined with sticky inflation expectations (traders now pricing 4%+ CPI probability) suggest real yields are moving HIGHER, a structural headwind for gold. Equity profit-taking provides some flight-to-safety support, but USD strength (evidenced by GBP weakness) is outweighing that. Fed Chair transition uncertainty (Warsh vote Wednesday) and Goolsbee's hawkish tone on services inflation reinforce tighter policy expectations.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh confirmation vote (Wednesday/Thursday, May 13-14)
PPI and crude oil inventory data due Wednesday per stock market calendar (18:00)
Powell's term ends May 15 — leadership void creates volatility
Trump-Xi summit ongoing (geopolitical risk could spike or ease)
Next CPI/PCE print (likely late May) — market now pricing hot inflation into June
screening.risk_warning: If Iran ceasefire suddenly materializes or Trump-Xi summit yields de-escalation, oil could collapse and safe-haven demand evaporates, removing the last prop under gold. Conversely, if Fed Chair Warsh signals a dovish pivot or real yields begin compressing (flight-to-quality in Treasuries), gold could reverse sharply higher. DXY weakness or surprise disinflation would also flip the bias bullish. Current bias is fragile with confidence only 5/10 due to conflicting forces and imminent policy events.
